explicit_text: I was born and raised in Denver, Colorado in 1926. I went to Lincoln Elementary, Byers Junior High, and South High School here. Denver was where I spent my childhood, walking to school and enjoying holidays like the Fourth of July at the Denver Country Club. I also remember going to theaters on South Broadway and living near Kalamath Street where Hurt and Company was located.

explicit_text: I was born in Colorado, specifically Denver, in 1926. One memorable trip I took was with my grandmother up to Grand Junction, Colorado, to help a friend pick peaches. Growing up in Colorado shaped much of my early life experiences, from school to family outings.

explicit_text: My dad worked for ACME, specifically at the docks. It was called ACME Fast Freight, and that was part of our family life growing up in Denver. His work there was a steady part of our household background.

explicit_text: I attended Lincoln Elementary School from first through sixth grade. I loved school, and I remember my art teacher, Nicetrom, who was great, though I didn’t like my English teacher very much. Walking to Lincoln Elementary was about six blocks from home, and it was an important part of my early education in Denver.

explicit_text: I attended South High School in Denver because all my family went there. It was located near University Avenue, and I remember the rationing during World War II affecting our school life. I enjoyed math there and took some science, though I wasn’t fond of the science teacher.

explicit_text: During the war, I took welding classes at Emily Griffith Technical College while still in school. I was only sixteen then, and the skills I learned there helped me get hired later. Emily Griffith was a place where many of us bettered ourselves during World War II.

explicit_text: South Broadway was where we went to the movies, especially to the Weber and Mayan Theatres. We lived near there and even delivered flyers along the block from Alameda to First Avenue to get free tickets. It was a lively part of my childhood entertainment in Denver.

explicit_text: Fourth of July was always a big celebration for us. My dad would buy a box of firecrackers, and we’d set them off, and we also went to the Denver Country Club to watch their fabulous fireworks. It was a joyful time with family and community in Denver.

explicit_text: Hurt and Company was just a half block from where I lived on Kalamath Street. After I graduated, I started working there, though I didn’t weld. It was close to home and part of my early work experience in Denver.
